title: Block 40 — Wire ActivateSportScreen to SportContext with Map navigation on sport selection

requirements.summary: Wire ActivateSportScreen to SportContext and navigate to Map on sport selection
acceptanceCriteria: ActivateSportScreen imports useSport from the SportContext file
The activateSport function from useSport is called with the selected sport name when a sport icon is tapped
After tapping any sport icon, the user is navigated to the Map screen/route
Sport icons for Soccer, Basketball, Tennis, Running, and Yoga each invoke onPickSport with their respective sport name
The component accepts a navigation prop (or equivalent router navigation) so the navigate call does not throw

file_operations.description: Import useSport hook from SportContext. Extract activateSport from the hook. Replace or augment the existing sport icon tap handler to call activateSport(sportName) and then use TanStack Router's useNavigate hook to navigate to '/map'. Ensure each sport icon (Soccer, Basketball, Tennis, Running, Yoga) invokes this handler with the correct sport name string.

requirements.summary: Ensure SportContext exists and SportProvider wraps the application root
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/context/SportContext.tsx exists and exports SportProvider and useSport
SportProvider is present in App.tsx wrapping the router/navigation tree
useSport hook throws a descriptive error if used outside SportProvider
activateSport(sport) sets currentSport, sets sportStatus to 'active', and sets userMode to 'Out Now'
deactivateSport() resets currentSport to null, sportStatus to 'inactive', and userMode to 'Planned'

file_operations.description: Create the SportContext file defining SportStatus type as 'inactive' | 'active', UserMode type as 'Out Now' | 'On My Way' | 'Planned', and SportContextValue interface with currentSport (string | null), sportStatus, userMode, locationEnabled (boolean), and functions activateSport(sport: string), deactivateSport(), setUserMode(mode: UserMode), and toggleLocation(). Export SportProvider component managing this state and useSport hook that throws an error if used outside the provider. Implement activateSport to set currentSport to the given sport, sportStatus to 'active', and userMode to 'Out Now'. Implement deactivateSport to reset currentSport to null, sportStatus to 'inactive', and userMode to 'Planned'.

Import SportProvider from the SportContext file. Wrap the existing RouterProvider (or the outermost router/navigation tree below QueryClientProvider) with SportProvider to make the context available throughout the application.
